title: Implement Add Questions flow, question type auto-detect, UI text fixes, logo + PDF rendering fixes

requirements.text: Fix mobile/desktop layouts so bottom action buttons never overflow outside the visible viewport on all affected screens (including Question Bank, AI Assistant, editor/preview/export screens). Ensure primary actions remain reachable without being covered by fixed/floating UI elements.

quotes: Fix bottom buttons going outside the page on all screens
Build now but don't change anything else

acceptanceCriteria: On common mobile viewport sizes (small/medium) and desktop, no page shows action buttons cut off below the fold due to fixed positioning or overflow issues.
Floating buttons (e.g., AI FAB) do not cover critical bottom CTAs; spacing/padding accounts for them when needed.
No horizontal scrolling is introduced as a side-effect of the fix.

requirements.text: Add a small "Add Questions" button at the top of the Question Bank screen and at the top of the Home screen, styled consistently with existing home quick-action buttons. The button must open the new Add Questions flow as a full-screen page.

quotes: Add small “Add Questions” button at top of Question Bank screen and also on home screen
1 full‑screen

acceptanceCriteria: Home screen shows a clearly visible, small "Add Questions" button near the top area (not only a floating button).
Question Bank screen shows a clearly visible, small "Add Questions" button near the top area.
Tapping either button navigates to the Add Questions page, which renders as a full-screen page (not a small dialog).

requirements.text: Create/enable a full-screen "Add Questions" flow that supports manual entry/paste of questions into the Question Bank without requiring the user to pre-select a question type before adding. The flow must save questions into the user’s personal Question Bank categorized by Board and Standard (Question Bank location), not into a paper section/heading.

quotes: Place questions automatically not in correct section/heading in correct question bank board, standard
add manual question entry and an Add Questions flow (home + question bank)

acceptanceCriteria: The Add Questions flow allows entering or pasting one or multiple questions without first choosing the question type.
Before saving to the personal bank, the flow captures Board and Standard (either user-selected or confirmed) and stores them on the created questions.
Newly added questions appear under the correct Question Bank board/standard listing in "My Questions" after saving.
The flow does not incorrectly route questions into a paper section/heading when the intent is Question Bank storage.

requirements.text: Implement immediate question-type auto-detection when a question is added/pasted in the Add Questions flow. After detection, prompt the user to confirm the detected type; if the user rejects it, require the user to choose the type for that question. Save each question with its confirmed question type.

quotes: 2 immediately
Make question type auto‑detect immediately when a question is added
If detection is correct: ask the user to confirm
If detection is wrong: ask type for each question

acceptanceCriteria: As soon as a question is added/pasted, detection runs automatically without an extra button click.
For each question, the UI shows the detected question type and asks the user to confirm or change it.
If the user indicates the detection is wrong, the UI forces selecting a type for that question before saving.
Saved questions reflect the confirmed type in the Question Bank filter (e.g., visible under the selected type).

requirements.text: Update per-screen informational/help text exactly as specified by the user: (1) Question Bank screen info text, (2) AI Assistant screen description text, (3) the screen that opens after selecting a standard: change the help text to the provided copy and replace the empty-state label "empty add questions" with the provided two-line message. Do not globally replace unrelated text on other screens.

quotes:
For question bank "Select a board and standard to browse available questions.
Add questions to sections to build your question paper."
For ai assistance "Create questions based on selected topics, standards, and preferences."
For screan which open after selecting standard "Find questions using question type"

And text writen on that page "empty add questions" replace it with
"No questions added yet.
Start by selecting add questions ."
not change all text with this change taxt according to work of that screen, it's function and all not all same

acceptanceCriteria: Question Bank screen shows exactly: "Select a board and standard to browse available questions. Add questions to sections to build your question paper." in its designated information/help area.
AI Assistant screen shows exactly: "Create questions based on selected topics, standards, and preferences." in its designated description/help area.
Board+Standard screen shows exactly: "Find questions using question type" in its designated help/description area.

When there are zero questions in the Board+Standard screen list, the empty state text is exactly:
"No questions added yet.
Start by selecting add questions ."
No unrelated labels or descriptions on other screens are overwritten with these strings.

requirements.text: Fix Teacher Profile logo upload: enable the disabled logo file input, allow selecting an image, persist it in the app’s stored profile data, and display the logo wherever the app shows institute/branding (profile view and paper editor header and export/print preview).

quotes: Fix logo upload in profile
Make institute name editable in paper editor and arrange logo + institute name layout properly

acceptanceCriteria: Profile screen’s "School Logo" file input is enabled and accepts image files.
After selecting a logo and saving, the logo persists across refresh (stored with the profile state mechanism already used by the app).
The logo is visible in the paper editor header area (where institute name/branding is displayed) and in export/print preview output.
If no logo is set, UI remains clean (no broken image icon).

requirements.text: Make institute name editable in the paper editor and fix the layout so the logo and institute name are arranged cleanly (no overlap, proper alignment) in the editor and in exported/printed output.

quotes: Make institute name editable in paper editor and arrange logo + institute name layout properly

acceptanceCriteria: Paper editor includes a clear way to edit institute name for the current paper (overriding profile defaults where applicable).
Logo and institute name render without overlap/cropping on small and large screens.
Export/print preview uses the edited institute name and renders it aligned with the logo.

requirements.text: Ensure images and tables in questions render correctly in export/print preview and print/PDF output. Images must auto-resize to fit within page margins while preserving aspect ratio, and tables must not be clipped. This applies to rich-cell images (e.g., match-pairs/table cells) and question attachments.

quotes: Ensure all images and tables show correctly in final PDF and print correctly
3 resize
Auto‑resize images to fit page

acceptanceCriteria: In export/print preview, rich content images and attachment images never overflow outside the printable area; they scale down to fit width and/or height constraints.
Printed/PDF output shows the same images/tables (no missing elements) and they are not clipped by page bounds.
Tables render with visible borders and do not break layout on narrow screens; print output remains readable.
Aspect ratio of images is preserved (no distortion).

constraints: Frontend immutable paths must not be edited: frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.tsx, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, frontend/src/components/ui (compose only).
Keep all backend logic (if any is added) within the single Motoko actor at backend/main.mo; only add backend state migration if existing stable state is changed.
Implement changes without altering unrelated screens/text/behavior beyond what is required by the listed requirements ("don't change anything else").
If implementation cannot be completed in a single delivery, split into two parts (milestones) while keeping the same acceptance criteria per requirement.
nonGoals: Adding third-party authentication providers (only Internet Identity is allowed).
Introducing external databases or non-IC backend services.
Integrating external AI/LLM APIs; AI generation remains mock/local unless already present.
